params.text:
What is expected of you

You do not have to run the whole complaints process, but you play a vital part in it. Your job is to make sure every complaint reaches the right people and that the person feels respected along the way.

  • Report every complaint — even one that seems trivial. What sounds minor to you may be part of a bigger pattern, and it is not your decision to filter it out.
  • Don’t promise outcomes — never guarantee a result, a refund or that someone will be disciplined. You cannot know where an investigation will lead.
  • Record and pass on the facts accurately and without adding your own judgement.
  • Be honest and open, and treat the person with dignity even when the conversation is difficult.
  • Know your procedure — be able to tell people how to complain and who to.

params.alt: Illustration for Your role as a care worker

params.taskDescription: Complete the description of the care worker’s role. Drag each word into the correct gap.
params.textField: Always *report* every complaint, even one that seems minor, because it is not your job to decide it does not matter. Never *promise* a particular outcome, since you cannot know where an investigation will lead. Record the *facts* accurately, treat the person with *dignity*, and know your complaints *procedure* so you can explain it.

params.question: If a complaint seems very minor, a care worker can decide not to pass it on.

l10n.wrongAnswerMessage: Not quite. You should report every complaint, however minor it seems — small issues can reveal bigger problems.
l10n.correctAnswerMessage: Correct. Every complaint should be reported. What looks minor may be part of a wider pattern, and filtering it out is not your decision.
